text:
Lady Gaga has officially kicked off the2024 Summer Olympicsin Paris, France.

On July 26,Gagaperformed the opening number as the world’s athletes made their way down the Seine in a parade of boats. Escorted down a grand staircase at Square Barye by a group of men and women carrying dramatic pink feathers, the 38-year-old singer was inspired by iconic French cabaret performances for her rendition of Zizi Jeanmaire’s “Mon Truc En Plume.”

While much of the performance was reminiscent of Jeanmaire’s ownperformances, Gaga’s custom Dior bodysuit and opera gloves were also giving sprinklings ofMarilyn MonroeinGentlemen Prefer Blondes(1953). You can watch for yourselfhere.

Lady Gaga performs at Square Barye during the athletes’ parade on the River Seine during the opening ceremony of the Olympic Games Paris 2024 on July 26, 2024 in Paris, France.

Is Lady Gaga French? No. Born Stefani Germanotta, Gaga grew up in an Italian American household in New York City. Does that matter? Also no. It’s the Paris Olympics.

Most viewers didn’t mind either. One user shared a Trinity The Tuck meme fromRuPaul’s Drag Race,quoting“I don't know what the fuck she’s saying but girl, I’m living.” That post quickly received over 5,000 likes.

“There’s one thing I would bet my life on and it’s that Lady Gaga will EGOT,” one user wrote onX.com. EGOT, of course, stands for “Emmy, Grammy, Oscar, and Tony.” She’s already halfway there with 13 Grammy Awards and one Oscar. To some, this performance alone deserves all four.

As one userwrote, “NO ONE WILL EVER DO IT LIKE LADY GAGA."



Gaga explained her song choice onX.comfollowing her performance. “I am also humbled to be asked by the Olympics organizing committee to sing such a special French song—a song to honor the French people and their tremendous history of art, music, and theatre,” she wrote, in part. “This song was sung by Zizi Jeanmaire, born in Paris a French ballerina, she famously sang ‘Mon Truc en Plumes’ in 1961. The title means ‘My Thing with Feathers.’ And this is not the first time we’ve crossed paths. Zizi starred in Cole Porter’s musical ‘Anything Goes’ which was my first jazz release.”

She continued, “Although I am not a French artist, I have always felt a very special connection with French people and singing French music—I wanted nothing more than to create a performance that would warm the heart of France, celebrate French art and music, and on such a momentous occasion remind everyone of one of the most magical cities on earth—Paris.”



For her performance, Lady Gaga says she borrowed feathered pom-poms from the French cabaret theater Le Lido and spent time “brushing up on some old skills.” She wrote, “I studied French choreography that put a modern twist on a French classic. I rehearsed tirelessly to study a joyful French dance, brushing up on some old skills—I bet you didn’t know I used to dance at a 60’s French party on the lower east side when I was first starting out!”

Lady Gaga performs at Square Barye during the athletes’ parade on the River Seine during the opening ceremony of the Olympic Games Paris 2024 on July 26, 2024 in Paris, France.

Gaga continued, “I hope you love this performance as much as I do. And to everyone in France, thank you so much for welcoming me to your country to sing in honor of you—it’s a gift I’ll never forget! Congratulations to all the athletes who are competing in this year’s Olympic Games! It is my supreme honor to sing for you and cheer you on!! Watching the Olympic Games always makes me cry! Your talent is unimaginable. Let the games begin!”

They’re athletic, yes, but these Olympians also have a talent for the ’Tok.

title: Lady Gaga Kicks Off the 2024 Summer Olympics With a French Cabaret Performance Worthy of an EGOT | Glamour
author: Emily Tannenbaum
description: Lady Gaga has officially kicked off the 2024 Summer Olympics in Paris, France, with a performance of Zizi Jeanmaire’s “Mon Truc En Plume.” Here's how fans responded.
